question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: For quick access-deny symptoms, check printer queue permissions for explicit Deny entries and remove if present (documented in TI-1623488)
context:
=== CITED SOURCES ===
SOURCE 3:
﻿
Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF60: Single user cannot print – others in same office without issue
Ticket number: TI-1623488
Company: Customer Company 60 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 20
Owner: Employee 27
Origin: Phone
Type: Incident
Created on: 02.12.25 09:03
Closure
Closure date: 03.12.25 10:21
Closure text: Permission on printer queue corrected according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Printing possible again.
Activities
02.12.25 08:55 – Phone report
Mr. Contact Person 20 reports that one employee in the Basel office cannot print documents.
All other employees in the same room can print without issue.
The affected user receives the message “Access denied” when attempting to print.
02.12.25 09:03
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 27.
02.12.25 09:20 – Initial analysis
Remote connection established with the affected user.
Printer “MFP-Basel-01” is visible.
When sending a print job, “Access denied” appears immediately.
Print job is not displayed in the queue.
02.12.25 09:45 – Comparison with another user
Test performed with colleague in the same office.
Printing works without restriction.
User account checked.
Membership in location group “PRT-Basel” confirmed.
02.12.25 10:30 – Further review
Comparison with “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”.
It specifies:
• Printer permissions are assigned exclusively via AD groups
• No individual user permissions at queue level
• NTFS and share permissions must be consistent
Review of the printer queue on the print server shows:
• AD group “PRT-Basel” has print permission
• Additionally, there is an explicit entry for the affected user with “Deny”
This entry originates from a previous test configuration.
02.12.25 11:10 – Test
Explicit deny permission removed.
User logged in again.
Test print successful.
03.12.25 09:40 – Customer feedback
Printing works normally again.
Additional tests within the team successful.
03.12.25 10:21 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 20
The root cause was an explicit “Deny” permission on the printer queue for the affected user.
The permission structure was cleaned up according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”.
Printing is now fully functional again.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company
is_rec_reason: Uses imperative directive ('check... and remove if present'), recommending an action to resolve access-deny symptoms.
groundedness_reason: Ticket TI-1623488 describes an 'Access denied' print error caused by an explicit Deny entry on the printer queue, and removing that Deny restored printing, directly supporting the recommendation.

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: Scan-to-Mail / SMTP relay: TI-1624175 (KF60)
context:
=== CITED SOURCES ===
SOURCE 6:
﻿
Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF60: Scan-to-Mail works only for internal recipients
Ticket number: TI-1624175
Company: Customer Company 60 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 25
Owner: Employee 30
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 10.12.25 08:41
Closure
Closure date: 18.12.25 16:04
Closure text: SMTP relay and DNS configuration adjusted according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Scan-to-Mail fully restored.
Activities
10.12.25 08:30 – Email from Contact Person 25
Good morning
At the Zurich location, we can scan from the multifunction device, but external recipients (e.g., Gmail or partner companies) do not receive the emails.
Internal recipients within our company receive the scans normally.
Printing continues to function without issue.
Could you please check this?
Kind regards
10.12.25 08:41
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 30.
10.12.25 09:15 – Initial analysis
Test scan to internal mailbox → successful.
Test scan to external address → no delivery.
No error message visible on the printer itself.
10.12.25 10:05 – Inquiry to customer
Have there been any recent changes to the mail environment or network?
10.12.25 10:40 – Customer response
Last week, the external mail security solution was adjusted.
Additionally, new DNS forwarding was configured at the location.
11.12.25 09:10 – Mail log analysis
Internal delivery occurs directly.
External delivery is not forwarded by the SMTP relay.
SMTP log shows: “Relay denied”.
11.12.25 11:30 – Comparison with blueprint
“Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”
Section Scan-to-Mail:
• Printers must not send directly to the internet
• Sending occurs exclusively via dedicated SMTP relay
• Relay allows only defined IP ranges
• DNS resolution must occur internally
• Authentication must not be bypassed
12.12.25 – Further analysis
Check of printer IP address.
Printer is located in newly defined printer VLAN.
This VLAN is not configured as an allowed range in the SMTP relay.
Internal routing works, but external relay is blocked.
15.12.25 – Coordination with Network and Mail Team
Confirmation that the new printer VLAN was not included in the relay allow list.
16.12.25 – Adjustment
SMTP relay configuration updated according to blueprint.
IP range of printer VLAN released.
17.12.25 – Test phase
Scan to internal mailbox → successful
Scan to external Gmail address → successful
Scan to partner company → successful
18.12.25 16:04 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 25
The root cause was an SMTP relay configuration that had not been updated after the introduction of a new printer VLAN.
The configuration was adjusted according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”.
Scan-to-Mail now works for both internal and external recipients.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company
is_rec_reason: This is a brief label/reference (Scan-to-Mail / SMTP relay: TI-1624175 (KF60)) and contains no recommendation, suggestion, or forward-looking instruction.
groundedness_reason: The CONTEXT shows ticket TI-1624175 (KF60) titled 'Scan-to-Mail works only for internal recipients' and documents SMTP relay 'Relay denied' and relay configuration adjustments restoring Scan-to-Mail, directly supporting the label.

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: If print jobs appear then disappear or do not reach the device, test network reachability: ping from the print server to the printer. In the documented Basel case, the print server could not ping the printer while a client in the printer VLAN could — indicating segmentation/firewall blocking between server and printer
context:
=== CITED SOURCES ===
SOURCE 22:
﻿
Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF60: Location printer Basel not responding to print jobs
Ticket number: TI-1622483
Company: Customer Company 60 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 15
Owner: Employee 21
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 06.11.25 08:37
Closure
Closure date: 12.11.25 14:52
Closure text: Network and queue configuration adjusted according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Function restored.
Activities
06.11.25 08:25 – Email from Contact Person 15
Good morning
Since yesterday, the printer “MFP-Basel-01” is no longer responding to print jobs. The documents remain in the queue, and in some cases disappear without being printed.
Other printers in the building are functioning normally.
Could you please check this?
Kind regards
06.11.25 08:37
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 21.
06.11.25 09:10 – Initial analysis
Remote access to an affected user.
Print job briefly appears in the print server queue, then disappears without printing.
Queue itself is not paused.
Print Spooler service is running.
Test print directly from the print server to the printer also fails.
06.11.25 10:05 – Inquiry to customer
Good day
Were there any recent changes to the network or infrastructure at the Basel location?
Thank you for your brief feedback.
06.11.25 11:20 – Customer response
Last week, a new network segment for devices was introduced. The printers were moved into a separate VLAN.
No other changes are known to us.
07.11.25 09:00 – Further analysis
Ping from print server to printer unsuccessful.
Ping from a client within the same location network successful.
This indicates segmentation between the server network and the printer VLAN.
Comparison with document “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”.
In the section Location Concept, it is defined that printers operate in a separate VLAN but must be reachable from the print server. Routing and firewall rules must be configured accordingly.
07.11.25 11:30 – Internal coordination with Network Team
Confirmation that the new firewall rule did not allow the server network access to the printer VLAN.
Rule adjusted according to standard.
10.11.25 – Test phase
Printing from print server successful.
Multiple test prints from user workstations performed.
No abnormalities observed.
12.11.25 14:40 – Customer feedback
Printer is functioning normally again.
Larger print jobs are also processed correctly.
12.11.25 14:52 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 15
The root cause was a missing network allowance between the print server and the newly configured printer VLAN at the Basel location.
The configuration was adjusted according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”. The printer is fully reachable again.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company
is_rec_reason: Contains conditional and directive advice ('If... then...', 'test network reachability: ping from the print server to the printer'), which is a recommendation for action.
groundedness_reason: The Basel ticket explicitly reports print jobs appearing then disappearing, shows the print server could not ping the printer while a client in the printer VLAN could, and concludes this indicates segmentation/firewall blocking—supporting the recommended ping test.

Created on: 14.01.25 08:17
Subject: Urgent: Emails remain in Outbox – KF22
From: Contact Person 08
To: HELPDESK, Consulting Sample Company
Hello everyone
Since this morning, emails are stuck in the Outbox for several users. In some cases, we receive the following error message:
0x800CCC0E – Connection to the server failed
SMTP server not responding
Approximately 12 employees from the Sales team are affected.
Internet connectivity is generally working.
Could you please check this urgently?
Kind regards
Contact Person 08
14.01.25 08:21
Ticket creation: TI-1548823 | KF22: Exchange Online – Emails remain in Outbox
Ticket notification sent to Contact Person 08
Priority: High
14.01.25 08:29
Ticket assigned: Employee 34
Status changes
14.01.25 08:35
Status: In Progress
Notes:
14.01.25 08:42 – Employee 34
Contact established with Contact Person 08.
Additional information:
• Issue affects only users with locally installed Outlook (Windows 11)
• Webmail (OWA) works
• Mobile devices (iOS) work
• Error occurs only when sending
• Receiving works
Initial assessment:
→ Likely SMTP authentication or Hybrid Connector issue
14.01.25 09:15 – Client-side troubleshooting
Actions performed:
• Recreated Outlook profile
• Removed and re-added account
• Checked SMTP port (587 with STARTTLS)
• Disabled antivirus SMTP scanning
• Checked client firewall
Result: No improvement
14.01.25 10:30 – Exchange Admin Center analysis
• Checked Mailflow → Queue Monitoring
• No global Microsoft 365 outage reported
• Performed Message Trace → no blocking visible
• SMTP AUTH globally disabled? → No
Suspicion: Hybrid environment with local Exchange relay
14.01.25 11:12 – Escalation
Ticket forwarded to System Engineer (Exchange Specialist).
Reason:
Possible misconfiguration in Hybrid Connector or SMTP Auth policy
14.01.25 11:15
Ticket no longer in your responsibility | TI-1548823
Forwarded to: BU Infrastructure Engineering
14.01.25 11:16
Ticket assigned: System Engineer 07
Further analysis (System Engineer)
15.01.25 08:55 – System Engineer 07
Hybrid setup analysis:
• Environment: Exchange Hybrid (OnPrem + M365)
• Send Connector checked
• TLS certificate checked
• SPF / DKIM unchanged
Log analysis on Exchange Server:
Event Log shows:
535 5.7.3 Authentication unsuccessful
SMTP AUTH was disabled for the affected users.
15.01.25 10:20 – Root cause identified
Tenant security policy was adjusted last week:
Security Default → enabled
Result: SMTP AUTH disabled for legacy clients
Sales team still uses older Outlook version (Build 2019 MSI).
15.01.25 11:05 – Actions
• SMTP AUTH selectively enabled for affected users
• Conditional Access Policy adjusted
• Test sending successful
16.01.25 09:00 – Monitoring
Mailflow monitored for 24 hours
No further errors in logs
21.01.25 14:12 – Customer feedback
All users can send normally again.
No further issues observed.
Ticket closure
22.01.25 16:42 – HELPDESK
Dear Contact Person 08
Your ticket dated 14.01.25 has been successfully closed.
Root cause:
SMTP AUTH was disabled due to new Security Default Policy.
Action taken:
Selective activation and adjustment of the Conditional Access Policy.
If you have any further questions, please do not hesitate to contact us.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company – Helpdesk
is_rec_reason: Uses imperative directive verbs ('Confirm', 'verify') instructing to perform checks, so it is a recommendation to take action.

Closure date: 18.12.25 16:04
Closure text: SMTP relay and DNS configuration adjusted according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Scan-to-Mail fully restored.
Activities
10.12.25 08:30 – Email from Contact Person 25
Good morning
At the Zurich location, we can scan from the multifunction device, but external recipients (e.g., Gmail or partner companies) do not receive the emails.

Printing continues to function without issue.
Could you please check this?
Kind regards
10.12.25 08:41
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 30.
10.12.25 09:15 – Initial analysis
Test scan to internal mailbox → successful.

No error message visible on the printer itself.
10.12.25 10:05 – Inquiry to customer
Have there been any recent changes to the mail environment or network?

Last week, the external mail security solution was adjusted.

11.12.25 09:10 – Mail log analysis
Internal delivery occurs directly.
External delivery is not forwarded by the SMTP relay.
SMTP log shows: “Relay denied”.
11.12.25 11:30 – Comparison with blueprint
“Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”
Section Scan-to-Mail:
• Printers must not send directly to the internet
• Sending occurs exclusively via dedicated SMTP relay

• DNS resolution must occur internally
• Authentication must not be bypassed

Check of printer IP address.
Printer is located in newly defined printer VLAN.
This VLAN is not configured as an allowed range in the SMTP relay.
Internal routing works, but external relay is blocked.
15.12.25 – Coordination with Network and Mail Team

SMTP relay configuration updated according to blueprint.
IP range of printer VLAN released.

Scan to internal mailbox → successful
Scan to external Gmail address → successful
Scan to partner company → successful
18.12.25 16:04 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 25
The root cause was an SMTP relay configuration that had not been updated after the introduction of a new printer VLAN.
The configuration was adjusted according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”.
Scan-to-Mail now works for both internal and external recipients.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company
is_rec_reason: Direct instruction using directive verbs ('Check', 'look for') telling the reader to perform actions to investigate SMTP relay logs.
groundedness_reason: The ticket's mail log analysis explicitly shows the SMTP log entry “Relay denied” and the activity notes include checking the printer IP/VLAN, so checking SMTP relay logs for the printer's IP and looking for relay rejections matches the documented symptom.

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: The “scan-to-mail: small scans work, big scans dropped” symptom is similar in part to a documented scan-to-mail incident where the printer VLAN was not allowed by the SMTP relay and external delivery was denied (“Relay denied”). The resolution there was to update the SMTP relay allow list for the printer VLAN and then re-test sending; after the change external scans were delivered again. This is documented in ticket TI-1624175 (KF60)

18.12.24 00:34 Re: TRAIN-CE/FO integration / MFA
Dear Employee 7,
During the joint analysis of the issue, we identified that two parameters were not correctly set during the ADFS client registration. In the new client application used specifically for the MFA request of the Toolbox on the Train system, the following parameters were set to true:
EnableJWT
RequestMFAFromClaimsProviders

EnableJWT activates the issuance of JSON Web Tokens in the authentication process. If enabled, ADFS issues JWTs instead of other token formats such as SAML. These tokens are evaluated by the target application. If the target system does not support JWTs or cannot validate their signature, authentication fails.

RequestMFAFromClaimsProviders forwards MFA requirements to configured claims providers. This delegates MFA execution to external providers and increases complexity in authentication flow, logging, and auditing.

The combination of these parameters can lead to login failures if the target system is not designed to handle JWTs or external MFA claims.

We have documented the configuration to avoid future issues. From our perspective, no further problems are expected after adjusting the parameters. If MFA works stably and the Train environment is reachable again, the login topic can be considered resolved.

Kind regards
Employee 21

18.12.24 00:34 Re: TRAIN-CE/FO integration / MFA
Dear Employee 7,
During the joint analysis of the issue, we identified that two parameters were not correctly set during the ADFS client registration. In the new client application used specifically for the MFA request of the Toolbox on the Train system, the following parameters were set to true:
EnableJWT
RequestMFAFromClaimsProviders

EnableJWT activates the issuance of JSON Web Tokens in the authentication process. If enabled, ADFS issues JWTs instead of other token formats such as SAML. These tokens are evaluated by the target application. If the target system does not support JWTs or cannot validate their signature, authentication fails.

RequestMFAFromClaimsProviders forwards MFA requirements to configured claims providers. This delegates MFA execution to external providers and increases complexity in authentication flow, logging, and auditing.

The combination of these parameters can lead to login failures if the target system is not designed to handle JWTs or external MFA claims.

We have documented the configuration to avoid future issues. From our perspective, no further problems are expected after adjusting the parameters. If MFA works stably and the Train environment is reachable again, the login topic can be considered resolved.

Kind regards
Employee 21

18.12.24 00:34 Re: TRAIN-CE/FO integration / MFA
Dear Employee 7,
During the joint analysis of the issue, we identified that two parameters were not correctly set during the ADFS client registration. In the new client application used specifically for the MFA request of the Toolbox on the Train system, the following parameters were set to true:
EnableJWT
RequestMFAFromClaimsProviders

EnableJWT activates the issuance of JSON Web Tokens in the authentication process. If enabled, ADFS issues JWTs instead of other token formats such as SAML. These tokens are evaluated by the target application. If the target system does not support JWTs or cannot validate their signature, authentication fails.

RequestMFAFromClaimsProviders forwards MFA requirements to configured claims providers. This delegates MFA execution to external providers and increases complexity in authentication flow, logging, and auditing.

The combination of these parameters can lead to login failures if the target system is not designed to handle JWTs or external MFA claims.

We have documented the configuration to avoid future issues. From our perspective, no further problems are expected after adjusting the parameters. If MFA works stably and the Train environment is reachable again, the login topic can be considered resolved.

Kind regards
Employee 21

18.12.24 00:34 Re: TRAIN-CE/FO integration / MFA
Dear Employee 7,
During the joint analysis of the issue, we identified that two parameters were not correctly set during the ADFS client registration. In the new client application used specifically for the MFA request of the Toolbox on the Train system, the following parameters were set to true:
EnableJWT
RequestMFAFromClaimsProviders

EnableJWT activates the issuance of JSON Web Tokens in the authentication process. If enabled, ADFS issues JWTs instead of other token formats such as SAML. These tokens are evaluated by the target application. If the target system does not support JWTs or cannot validate their signature, authentication fails.

RequestMFAFromClaimsProviders forwards MFA requirements to configured claims providers. This delegates MFA execution to external providers and increases complexity in authentication flow, logging, and auditing.

The combination of these parameters can lead to login failures if the target system is not designed to handle JWTs or external MFA claims.

We have documented the configuration to avoid future issues. From our perspective, no further problems are expected after adjusting the parameters. If MFA works stably and the Train environment is reachable again, the login topic can be considered resolved.

Kind regards
Employee 21

Dear Employee 14,
Your ticket from 26.11.24 10:07 has been closed.
Closure description:
Parameters adjusted on ADFS as in the TEST environment.

18.12.24 00:34 Re: TRAIN-CE/FO integration / MFA
Dear Employee 7,
During the joint analysis of the issue, we identified that two parameters were not correctly set during the ADFS client registration. In the new client application used specifically for the MFA request of the Toolbox on the Train system, the following parameters were set to true:
EnableJWT
RequestMFAFromClaimsProviders

EnableJWT activates the issuance of JSON Web Tokens in the authentication process. If enabled, ADFS issues JWTs instead of other token formats such as SAML. These tokens are evaluated by the target application. If the target system does not support JWTs or cannot validate their signature, authentication fails.

RequestMFAFromClaimsProviders forwards MFA requirements to configured claims providers. This delegates MFA execution to external providers and increases complexity in authentication flow, logging, and auditing.

The combination of these parameters can lead to login failures if the target system is not designed to handle JWTs or external MFA claims.

We have documented the configuration to avoid future issues. From our perspective, no further problems are expected after adjusting the parameters. If MFA works stably and the Train environment is reachable again, the login topic can be considered resolved.

Kind regards
Employee 21

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: What the records document: external users can lose effective access when two groups with identical names exist (one Cloud, one synchronized On‑Prem). SharePoint evaluates group membership by objectId; synchronized On‑Prem groups can overwrite cloud groups and drop external guests that were only in the cloud group. Consolidation and full Azure AD Connect sync fixed that scenario and restored external access in the documented incident

• External guests exist only in Cloud group
• SharePoint references the synchronized On-Prem group

10.04.25 11:25

Cloud group:
→ Contains external guests
Synchronized On-Prem group:
→ Contains only internal users

11.04.25 08:30
Reproduction in test:
• On-Prem group re-synchronized
• Cloud group overwritten
• External users reproducibly lose access
Root Cause
Two groups existed with identical name:
COMPLIANCE_TEAM
1. Cloud-native group
2. Synchronized On-Prem group
Through Azure AD Connect, the Cloud group was gradually overwritten or deactivated.
SharePoint referenced the synchronized version.
External guests were never managed On-Prem and therefore were not included in the synchronized group.
Actions
11.04.25 – 14.04.25
• Consolidation of group structure
• Old Cloud group removed
• On-Prem group properly cleaned
• External guests managed as Cloud-only members
• Azure AD Connect full synchronization performed
15.04.25 – Monitoring
Access remains stable
No further inconsistencies
17.04.25 15:18 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 07
The root cause was the parallel existence of two groups with identical names but different origins (Cloud vs. On-Prem synchronized).
Due to synchronization, effective group membership for external users was not correctly applied.
The group structure was consolidated and fully synchronized.
Access is now stable.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

Access denied or item not found

• User fully logged out (Office + browser)
• Browser cache cleared
• Device compliance status checked

The root cause was not group nesting, but a delay in Conditional Access policy evaluation combined with cached authentication tokens.

Ticket number: TI-1604879

Activities
22.07.25 09:02 – Email from Contact Person 16 to Helpdesk
Good morning
Since yesterday, several employees report that external calls in Microsoft Teams disconnect after approximately 30 seconds.
The call is established normally, both parties can hear each other, but the connection is terminated after a short time.
Internal Teams calls are not affected.
Could you please review this?
Kind regards
22.07.25 09:14
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 35.
22.07.25 10:05 – Helpdesk inquiry
Good day
Thank you for your report.
Could you please confirm:
– Does it affect incoming and outgoing calls?
– Does the disconnection always occur after approximately the same duration?
– Are all locations affected or only a specific one?
Thank you very much.
22.07.25 10:42 – Customer response
It only affects the Basel location.
Both incoming and outgoing calls.
The disconnection occurs almost exactly after 30 seconds, sometimes slightly earlier.
Home office users are not affected.
22.07.25 11:30 – Internal note
Test performed with affected user.
External call to mobile phone.
Call disconnected after 29–31 seconds.
No error message in the Teams client, simply “Call Ended.”
23.07.25 08:55 – Initial hypothesis
Possible client error or unstable connection.
Test with another device → same behavior.
Test with LAN cable instead of docking station → no change.
23.07.25 11:10 – Email to customer
Good day
We are currently analyzing the Direct Routing logs.
Signaling is established correctly.
The disconnection does not appear to be triggered by the client.
Were there any recent network changes at the Basel location?
Kind regards
23.07.25 13:45 – Customer response
On Friday, a new network segment for VoIP traffic was implemented.
A separate VLAN for “Voice” was created.
Could this be relevant?
24.07.25 09:05 – Internal analysis
SBC logs reviewed:
– SIP INVITE successful
– 200 OK correct
– Call established
– After 30 seconds, BYE from SBC
No obvious error code.
24.07.25 10:30 – Firewall analysis
No blocking visible.
Ports open according to standard configuration.
24.07.25 14:20 – Detailed SIP analysis
Session-Expires header reviewed.
Session timer set to 30 seconds.
Comparison with other locations shows:
There, 180 seconds.
25.07.25 08:50 – Review of internal documentation
Document:
“Blueprint – Microsoft Teams Direct Routing & Session Handling – Version 2.1”
Section on NAT session persistence and SIP session timers relevant.
Note in the blueprint:
For certain firewall or NAT configurations, the session timer must be adjusted to avoid premature termination.
25.07.25 11:15 – Internal tests
Temporary adjustment of session parameters on SBC.
Test call performed.
Call remains stable for several minutes.
Reverting to original configuration → disconnection after approx. 30 seconds reproducible.
28.07.25 – Coordination with network team
New “Voice” VLAN uses different NAT policy.
UDP session timeout configured to 30 seconds.
Comparison with Zurich location → 180 seconds.
29.07.25 – Adjustment
NAT session timeout adjusted according to architecture specification.
Details not documented in ticket for security reasons.
30.07.25 – Customer feedback
Good morning
We conducted several test calls.
No more disconnections observed.
Longer conversations are also stable.
Thank you very much.
31.07.25 15:52 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 16
The root cause was a deviation in the session and NAT configuration at the Basel location related to the new VLAN structure.
The configuration was adjusted according to the internal Direct Routing architecture.
Calls now remain stable.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

groundedness_reason: The cited ticket TI-1604879 documents external calls at the Basel location dropping after ~30s, identifies a 30s NAT/UDP session timeout and a 30s SIP session timer on the SBC, and records adjustments to NAT/session parameters and the SBC session timer which resolved the issue.

03.09.25 09:40 – Helpdesk inquiry

03.09.25 11:15 – Internal note

Internal call → works.
External call → immediate termination after dialing.
Teams briefly shows “Connecting”, then “Call ended”.
04.09.25 08:50 – Email to customer
Good morning
We are currently reviewing the voice routing and license configuration.
Can you confirm whether these users were recently moved to another team or security group?

04.09.25 09:30 – Customer response

• Teams license

05.09.25 11:20 – Email to customer

Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – External calls disconnect after approx. 30 seconds
Ticket number: TI-1604879
Company: Customer Company 52 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 16
Owner: Employee 35
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 22.07.25 09:14
Closure
Closure date: 31.07.25 15:52
Closure text: Analysis completed. Adjustment made according to Direct Routing architecture. Calls stable.
Activities
22.07.25 09:02 – Email from Contact Person 16 to Helpdesk
Good morning
Since yesterday, several employees report that external calls in Microsoft Teams disconnect after approximately 30 seconds.
The call is established normally, both parties can hear each other, but the connection is terminated after a short time.
Internal Teams calls are not affected.
Could you please review this?
Kind regards
22.07.25 09:14
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 35.
22.07.25 10:05 – Helpdesk inquiry
Good day
Thank you for your report.
Could you please confirm:
– Does it affect incoming and outgoing calls?
– Does the disconnection always occur after approximately the same duration?
– Are all locations affected or only a specific one?
Thank you very much.
22.07.25 10:42 – Customer response
It only affects the Basel location.
Both incoming and outgoing calls.
The disconnection occurs almost exactly after 30 seconds, sometimes slightly earlier.
Home office users are not affected.
22.07.25 11:30 – Internal note
Test performed with affected user.
External call to mobile phone.
Call disconnected after 29–31 seconds.
No error message in the Teams client, simply “Call Ended.”
23.07.25 08:55 – Initial hypothesis
Possible client error or unstable connection.
Test with another device → same behavior.
Test with LAN cable instead of docking station → no change.
23.07.25 11:10 – Email to customer
Good day
We are currently analyzing the Direct Routing logs.
Signaling is established correctly.
The disconnection does not appear to be triggered by the client.
Were there any recent network changes at the Basel location?
Kind regards
23.07.25 13:45 – Customer response
On Friday, a new network segment for VoIP traffic was implemented.
A separate VLAN for “Voice” was created.
Could this be relevant?
24.07.25 09:05 – Internal analysis
SBC logs reviewed:
– SIP INVITE successful
– 200 OK correct
– Call established
– After 30 seconds, BYE from SBC
No obvious error code.
24.07.25 10:30 – Firewall analysis
No blocking visible.
Ports open according to standard configuration.
24.07.25 14:20 – Detailed SIP analysis
Session-Expires header reviewed.
Session timer set to 30 seconds.
Comparison with other locations shows:
There, 180 seconds.
25.07.25 08:50 – Review of internal documentation
Document:
“Blueprint – Microsoft Teams Direct Routing & Session Handling – Version 2.1”
Section on NAT session persistence and SIP session timers relevant.
Note in the blueprint:
For certain firewall or NAT configurations, the session timer must be adjusted to avoid premature termination.
25.07.25 11:15 – Internal tests
Temporary adjustment of session parameters on SBC.
Test call performed.
Call remains stable for several minutes.
Reverting to original configuration → disconnection after approx. 30 seconds reproducible.
28.07.25 – Coordination with network team
New “Voice” VLAN uses different NAT policy.
UDP session timeout configured to 30 seconds.
Comparison with Zurich location → 180 seconds.
29.07.25 – Adjustment
NAT session timeout adjusted according to architecture specification.
Details not documented in ticket for security reasons.
30.07.25 – Customer feedback
Good morning
We conducted several test calls.
No more disconnections observed.
Longer conversations are also stable.
Thank you very much.
31.07.25 15:52 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 16
The root cause was a deviation in the session and NAT configuration at the Basel location related to the new VLAN structure.
The configuration was adjusted according to the internal Direct Routing architecture.
Calls now remain stable.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – External number cannot be dialed
Ticket number: TI-1609823
Company: Customer Company 52 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 21
Owner: Employee 39
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 03.09.25 09:08
Closure
Closure date: 12.09.25 15:40
Closure text: Analysis completed. Routing configuration adjusted according to Teams Voice Architecture. Dialing external numbers possible again.
Activities
03.09.25 08:55 – Email from Contact Person 21
Good morning
Some employees have been unable to dial external phone numbers since today.
Internal Teams calls function normally.
External calls are terminated immediately; in some cases the message “Call failed” appears.
Could you please check this?
Kind regards
03.09.25 09:08
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 39.
03.09.25 09:40 – Helpdesk inquiry
Good day
Are all users affected or only specific ones?
Does it affect all external numbers or only certain ones?
Were there any recent changes to licenses or user roles?
Thank you for your feedback.
03.09.25 10:25 – Customer response
It affects about 6 users, all from the Sales team.
Other teams can call normally.
It affects all external numbers.
We are not aware of any license changes.
03.09.25 11:15 – Internal note
Test performed with affected user.
Internal call → works.
External call → immediate termination after dialing.
Teams briefly shows “Connecting”, then “Call ended”.
04.09.25 08:50 – Email to customer
Good morning
We are currently reviewing the voice routing and license configuration.
Can you confirm whether these users were recently moved to another team or security group?
Kind regards
04.09.25 09:30 – Customer response
Yes, the affected users were organizationally reassigned to the new department “Enterprise Sales” last week.
New Azure AD groups were created for this purpose.
04.09.25 11:40 – Teams Admin Center analysis
Users have:
• Teams license
• Calling Plan not active
• Direct Routing configured
Observation:
Voice Routing Policy = “Global”
Not “DR-Sales”
Comparison with working user from another team shows:
Voice Routing Policy = “DR-Standard”
05.09.25 09:05 – Internal review
Get-CsOnlineVoiceRoutingPolicy shows that policy “Global” does not reference any PSTN Usage.
Policy “DR-Standard” includes PSTN Usage “External-Route”.
05.09.25 11:20 – Email to customer
Good day
We have identified that the affected users are currently assigned a different Voice Routing Policy than the other employees.
We are reviewing why this assignment was changed.
Kind regards
08.09.25 – Internal analysis
New Azure AD group “Enterprise-Sales” was created.
Automated provisioning script assigns policy “Global” by default when users become members of this group.
The internal document
“Blueprint – Teams Voice Routing & Policy Assignment – Version 1.3”
states that users with Direct Routing must be assigned a dedicated routing policy.
09.09.25 – Test phase
Temporary assignment of policy “DR-Standard” to affected user.
Test call to mobile phone performed.
Call successful.
Reverted to “Global” → issue reproducible.
10.09.25 – Coordination with Identity team
Provisioning script was modified two weeks ago.
New group structure was not reflected in the routing blueprint.
11.09.25 – Adjustment
Policy assignment corrected according to Voice Routing architecture.
Provisioning logic adjusted.
12.09.25 – Customer feedback
Good day
All affected users can dial external numbers again.
Longer calls also function normally.
Thank you for your support.
12.09.25 15:40 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 21
The root cause was an incorrect Voice Routing Policy assignment following an organizational restructuring.
The routing configuration was adjusted according to the internal Teams Voice Architecture.
External calls are now possible again.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

Ticket number: TI-1612036

Contact person: Contact Person 22
Owner: Employee 41
Origin: Email
Type: Incident

Closure
Closure date: 29.09.25 16:18
Closure text: Analysis completed. Routing and translation rules adjusted according to Direct Routing Blueprint. Incoming calls fully functional again.
Activities
18.09.25 08:40 – Email from Contact Person 22
Good morning
We have noticed that incoming calls from certain customers are no longer reaching us.
Callers report that they either receive a busy tone or the connection is disconnected after a few seconds.
Other external calls are functioning normally.
Could you please check this?
Kind regards
18.09.25 08:56
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 41.
18.09.25 09:25 – Helpdesk inquiry
Good day
Could you please provide the following information:
– Example phone numbers where the issue occurs
– Since when the issue has existed
– Whether all users are affected or only specific ones
Thank you.
18.09.25 10:15 – Customer response
Here are two examples:
+41 44 785 23 10
+41 44 785 23 11
Other numbers such as +41 31 … work without issue.
The problem has existed since last week.
All employees are affected.
18.09.25 11:40 – Internal analysis
Test call from private mobile (different number range) → works.
Test with number in specified range → call not delivered.
In the Teams Admin Center, no incoming call is shown.
19.09.25 09:05 – Initial hypothesis
Possible provider issue.
Carrier contacted.
19.09.25 14:20 – Carrier feedback
From the carrier’s perspective, the call is correctly delivered to the company’s SBC.
No routing errors visible on their side.
22.09.25 08:55 – SBC log analysis
For functioning numbers:
INVITE accepted and forwarded to Teams.
For affected numbers:
INVITE received but immediately answered with “404 Not Found”.
22.09.25 11:30 – Email to customer
Good day
We see that incoming calls from the provider are reaching the SBC.
However, the requests are not being correctly forwarded internally.
Were there recent changes to number ranges or new extensions?
Kind regards
22.09.25 13:05 – Customer response
Yes, we received a new number range from the provider last week and partially migrated old numbers.
The affected numbers belong to this new range.
23.09.25 09:10 – Internal review
Comparison with document
“Blueprint – Microsoft Teams Direct Routing & Number Translation – Version 2.3”
Section regarding:
• Normalization of incoming numbers
• E.164 format
• Translation rules on SBC
• Voice routing policies
Finding:
New numbers are not included in the SBC translation table.
Routing rule covers only the old number range.
24.09.25 – Test
Temporary adjustment of translation rule for new number range.
Test call performed.
Call correctly signaled and delivered.
Reverting to original configuration → issue reproducible.
25.09.25 – Coordination with customer
Confirmation that new number range was fully migrated.
Internal documentation had not yet been updated.
26.09.25 – Adjustment
Routing and translation rules extended according to architectural specification.
Details not documented in ticket for security reasons.
29.09.25 – Customer feedback
All tested numbers are working again.
Multiple customers also confirmed successful calls.
Thank you for the support.
29.09.25 16:18 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 22
The root cause was an inconsistent translation and routing configuration following the introduction of a new number range.
The configuration was adjusted according to the internal Direct Routing architecture.
Incoming calls are now correctly delivered again.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

22.07.25 10:05 – Helpdesk inquiry
Good day

22.07.25 10:42 – Customer response

Both incoming and outgoing calls.
The disconnection occurs almost exactly after 30 seconds, sometimes slightly earlier.
Home office users are not affected.
22.07.25 11:30 – Internal note
Test performed with affected user.
External call to mobile phone.
Call disconnected after 29–31 seconds.
No error message in the Teams client, simply “Call Ended.”
23.07.25 08:55 – Initial hypothesis
Possible client error or unstable connection.
Test with another device → same behavior.
Test with LAN cable instead of docking station → no change.
23.07.25 11:10 – Email to customer
Good day

Signaling is established correctly.
The disconnection does not appear to be triggered by the client.
Were there any recent network changes at the Basel location?

– SIP INVITE successful
– 200 OK correct
– Call established
– After 30 seconds, BYE from SBC

Session timer set to 30 seconds.

There, 180 seconds.

Document:
“Blueprint – Microsoft Teams Direct Routing & Session Handling – Version 2.1”
Section on NAT session persistence and SIP session timers relevant.
Note in the blueprint:
For certain firewall or NAT configurations, the session timer must be adjusted to avoid premature termination.
25.07.25 11:15 – Internal tests
Temporary adjustment of session parameters on SBC.
Test call performed.
Call remains stable for several minutes.
Reverting to original configuration → disconnection after approx. 30 seconds reproducible.
28.07.25 – Coordination with network team
New “Voice” VLAN uses different NAT policy.
UDP session timeout configured to 30 seconds.
Comparison with Zurich location → 180 seconds.
29.07.25 – Adjustment
NAT session timeout adjusted according to architecture specification.
Details not documented in ticket for security reasons.
30.07.25 – Customer feedback
Good morning
We conducted several test calls.
No more disconnections observed.
Longer conversations are also stable.
Thank you very much.
31.07.25 15:52 – Ticket closure

Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: What the records explicitly document: This is the same symptom as KF52: Microsoft Teams – External calls disconnect after approx. 30 seconds (TI-1604879). The documented root cause there was a Session-Expires / NAT session timeout mismatch: a new Voice VLAN had UDP NAT session timeout set to 30s while other sites used 180s. The documented remediation was to adjust the NAT/session timeout per the Direct Routing architecture (SBC session parameters and NAT timeout), after which calls remained stable

There, 180 seconds.
